notes: United Kingdom government policy publications. GOV.UK is the single domain for government services and information.

data.description: UK's data protection law. Incorporates the EU GDPR as UK GDPR via the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. Supplemented by national provisions covering law enforcement, intelligence services, and exceptions. Enforced by the ICO.
data.notes: Post-Brexit divergence pending via the UK Data (Use and Access) Bill 2024.

data.description: UK law imposing duties of care on online platforms regarding illegal content and content harmful to children. Enforced by Ofcom.
data.notes: Phased commencement; risk-assessment and child-safety duties active 2025.

data.description: UK's foundational computer-crime law. Criminalises unauthorised access (Section 1), unauthorised access with intent to commit further offences (Section 2), unauthorised modification (Section 3), and making/supplying tools for offences (Section 3A).
data.notes: Reform consultation ongoing (Home Office, 2023+) — possible new defence for legitimate security researchers.

match_nodes.description: Network and Information Systems Regulations 2018 (UK NIS)

data.description: UK implementation of the original EU NIS Directive (now superseded in EU by NIS2). Covers Operators of Essential Services (OES) and Relevant Digital Service Providers (RDSPs).
data.notes: Pending modernisation via the UK Cyber Security and Resilience Bill announced 2024.

data.description: UK government plan for AI adoption and governance.
data.notes: Published January 2025. Policy direction, not binding legislation. Based on Matt Clifford's independent review. Covers AI infrastructure (compute, data), skills and talent, government adoption of AI, and regulatory approach. Emphasizes pro-innovation regulatory framework rather than comprehensive AI legislation.
